name: Back to the Future
description: The Back to the Future trilogy — Marty, Doc, the DeLorean, and 1.21 gigawatts.

questions.q: What make and model of car is the famous time machine in Back to the Future?

source.notes: DMC DeLorean DMC-12, built 1981-83.

questions.q: Who stars as teenager Marty McFly?

source.notes: Fox was also on Family Ties simultaneously.

questions.q: What is the name of Marty's fictional California hometown?

source.notes: Central square with the clock tower.

questions.q: How much electrical power does the time circuit require to send the DeLorean through time?

source.notes: Doc pronounces it 'jigawatts.'

questions.q: How fast must the DeLorean travel to activate the flux capacitor and time-travel?

source.notes: Pick for speed with a 'nice-looking' speedometer.

questions.q: How many films are in the Back to the Future trilogy?

source.notes: Parts II and III were filmed back-to-back.

questions.q: In the first film, Marty accidentally travels to which decade?

source.notes: November 5, 1955 — 30 years before 1985.

questions.q: Who directed all three Back to the Future films?

source.notes: Spielberg produced; Zemeckis directed.

questions.q: Which actor plays eccentric inventor Dr. Emmett 'Doc' Brown?

source.notes: Lloyd had to stay hunched over to minimize height difference with Fox.

questions.q: Marty plays lead guitar in a rock band. What is its name?

source.notes: They audition with 'The Power of Love.'

questions.q: Which Huey Lewis and the News song plays over the audition scene and the film's opening?

source.notes: Lewis himself briefly cameos as the audition judge.

questions.q: In the DeLorean opening chase of the first film, who is pursuing Doc for his stolen plutonium?

source.notes: The film says Libyan nationalists — source document was wrong.

questions.q: Who plays Marty's perpetually bullied father George McFly?

source.notes: Glover was recast in the sequels; lawsuit followed.

questions.q: Who plays the town bully Biff Tannen in all three films?

source.notes: Wilson also voices Biff in most related media.

questions.q: Which 1980s teen heartthrob plays Marty's girlfriend Jennifer in the first film?

source.notes: Replaced by Elisabeth Shue in Parts II and III.

questions.q: What is the name of Doc Brown's golden-retriever-ish dog, who becomes the first living thing to travel through time?

source.notes: Precedes him is earlier Copernicus — Einstein is the time-travel dog.

questions.q: The film's score was composed by which Zemeckis collaborator, later known for The Avengers theme?

source.notes: Silvestri and Zemeckis have collaborated on nearly every film.

questions.q: How did Marty and Doc arrange to meet in 1955 so they could send Marty home?
choices: A letter left with the town clerk

source.notes: Marty explains everything to young Doc; they plan around the lightning strike.

questions.q: Which cinematic moment in 1955 provides the 1.21 gigawatts to send the DeLorean home?
choices: A lightning strike on the clock tower

source.notes: Mr. Fusion is Part II; lightning is the Part I finale.

questions.q: Which up-and-coming filmmaker was an executive producer on the trilogy?

source.notes: Through Amblin Entertainment.

questions.q: Part II sends Marty forward to which future year?

source.notes: October 21, 2015 — widely celebrated as 'BTTF Day.'

questions.q: Part III relocates Marty and Doc to which earlier time period?

source.notes: 1885, Hill Valley.
